reviews.review_msg: Nilsson is an engaging professor who used video clips from Monty Python (among others) to compare to the texts read in class. He encouraged variation in course assignments rather than just writing an essay/response essay. This class was mostly seminary and as long as you were paying attention it was pretty easy.

Professor Nilsson is one of the best profs I've ever had! He is so happy to listen to what students have to say, and totally encourages discussion. Also, the way he lays out his course helps you avoid the end of term paper crunch. He is so knowledgeable about gender, and the books he chooses are AMAZING. I would take any course with him again!!

Dr. Nilsson is a GREAT guy. Endlessly helpful and always accessible, willing to meet with you on his own time outside office hours to help you with your paper or give you valuable feedback. The discussions in this course were extremely interesting and crucial to the content and assignments. Really cares about what he's doing.

Fair marker, not too hard but not too easy either. Lousy incoherent lectures though, only like 2/3 of the class was there every day. Once insisted the Wizard of Oz was a metaphor advocating the 1930's New Deal even though it was written by a Republican in the 1890's. That really annoyed me

Overall, Nilsson is a great teacher. He's attentive, respectful, and constant in the face of adversity. . . kind of like Griselda, actually. Hm. He does tend to ask questions a lot that are extremely obvious and it's almost tiresome to raise your  hand and answer them. Sometimes I wished for a bit more, more stimulation, deeper analysis in class.

Absolutely outstanding prof. I ended up having him for 5 classes, and to be honest, I wish I could have taken more with him. These comments are all bang-on. He will never make you feel wrong if you raise your hand with an answer. Caring prof, wants to help you. Go to his office hours and pick his brain for your essays & you'll get excellent marks.

A truly compassionate guy with an interest in assorted media. Perhaps not as exciting as a medeival lit class should have been, but Joakim is willing to help. I agree with the comment that he is a bit "bumbling" :)Too much discussion;has a tendency to ask questions that are so painfully obvious that no response comes...to which he simply rephrases!

A bit bumbling, would really have liked a bit more structure to his lectures; he relied pretty heavily on class discussion, which is hard in a group of 60+. Reasonably fair marker and very easygoing guy. If you come to class with a couple of killer comments and work hard on essays you'll do fine.

Nilsson has the uncanny ability to take any interpretation and/or suggestions thrown at him, and not only run with it, but back it up with secondary sources off the top of his head. Excellent prof, hope he gets tenured.
Solid prof. Readily available for extra help and is very supportive and encouraging with all students. Enthusastic about course content and runs a very engaging seminar and lecture.
